concepts.name: Tree Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes connected by edges.

concepts.name: Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A tree where each node has at most two children, referred to as the left child and the right child.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a tree, containing data and references to its children.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.short_definition: The topmost node in a tree, from which all other nodes descend.

concepts.name: Queue Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A First-In, First-Out (FIFO) data structure used for managing elements in a specific order.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores nodes level by level.

concepts.name: Level Order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting nodes of a tree level by level, from left to right.

concepts.name: Tree Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a tree exactly once.

concepts.name: Parent Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that has one or more child nodes.

concepts.name: Child Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that is directly connected to another node (its parent) and is below it in the tree hierarchy.
